definitions.definition: A network of rivers, streams, and lakes that drain water from a particular area of land into an ocean, sea, or lake.
The area of land that drains water into a particular river or stream, forming a unique network of waterways.
A natural flowing body of water that drains water from its catchment area into an ocean, sea, or lake.
A smaller natural flowing body of water that drains into a larger river or ocean.
A body of freshwater that is surrounded by land and does not flow into the ocean.
The area of land that separates two drainage basins and prevents water from flowing between them.
The area of land that drains water into a particular river, stream, or lake.
A network of interconnected rivers, streams, and lakes that drain water from a particular area of land.
Streams that flow only during periods of heavy rainfall and dry up during the rest of the year.
Streams that flow throughout the year and do not dry up.
The area of land that drains water into a particular river, including its tributaries and surrounding landscape.
A network of interconnected rivers and streams that drain water from a particular area of land.
A smaller river or stream that flows into a larger river or ocean.
A landform created by the deposit of sediment at the mouth of a river, often forming a network of distributaries.
Branches of a river that flow away from the main river and into a delta or other waterbody.
A type of river course where the river flows in a winding or curved path.
A type of river course where the river erodes the surrounding landscape and creates a steep, V-shaped valley.
The process of wearing away or removal of soil, rock, or other materials from the Earth's surface by natural forces such as water, wind, or ice.
The process of depositing or settling of sediment, such as sand or silt, in a new location, often at the mouth of a river.
